ead.archdesc:
======= RMA04164School of Hotel Administration photographs and ephemera28-10-4164Compiled by RMC StaffDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
May 20152B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
rghisThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:31:30 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor) >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
School of Hotel Administration photographs and ephemera
Cornell University. School of Hotel Administration
28-10-4164
4.8 cubic feet. 4.8 cubic feet.
1920-2015. Photographs, publications, newspapers. Includes photographs, publications and newspaper clippings concerning the Statler Hotel and School of Hotel Administration. Collection material in English 8762807
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
RMC Staff
Date completed:
July 2015
EAD encoding:
RMC Staff, July 2015
Date modified:
RMC Staff, May 2018
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Includes photographs, publications and newspaper clippings concerning the Statler Hotel and School of Hotel Administration.
RELATED MATERIALS
See also the research material collected for the book Hospitality Leadership: Cornell Hotel School, #28-12-3774.
SERIES LIST
Series I. History Boxes 1, 2 and 3
Series II. Photographs Boxes 1, 4, 5
Series III. Slides Boxes 4.5
Series IV. "Life is Service" History of the Hotel School written in 1995 Boxes 2
Cite As:
School of Hotel Administration Photographs and Ephemera, #28-10-4164. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Hospitality industry. Hotel management schools -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. Cornell University -- : Faculty.
